2603.14586.title: The Scenic Route to Deception: Dark Patterns and Explainability Pitfalls in Conversational Navigation
2603.14586.paper_summary: As pedestrian navigation increasingly experiments with Generative AI, and in particular Large Language Models, the nature of routing risks transforming from a verifiable geometric task into an opaque, persuasive dialogue. While conversational interfaces promise personalisation, they introduce risks of manipulation and misplaced trust. We categorise these risks using a 2x2 framework based on intent and origin, distinguishing between intentional manipulations (dark patterns) and unintended harms (explainability pitfalls). We propose seamful design strategies to mitigate these harms. We suggest that one robust way to operationalise trustworthy conversational navigation is through neuro-symbolic architecture, where verifiable pathfinding algorithms ground GenAI's persuasive capabilities, ensuring systems explain their limitations and incentives as clearly as they explain the route.
2603.14586.paper_summary_zh: 隨著行人導航越來越多地實驗生成式人工智慧，特別是大型語言模型，路徑規劃的性質風險將從可驗證的幾何任務轉變為不透明的、具有說服力的對話。雖然對話介面承諾個性化，但它們也引入了操控和錯誤信任的風險。我們使用基於意圖和來源的 2x2 框架來對這些風險進行分類，區分故意操控（黑暗模式）和無意的傷害（可解釋性陷阱）。我們提出了無縫設計策略來減輕這些傷害。我們建議，一種將可信的對話式導航實現為可操作的方法是通過神經符號架構，讓可驗證的路徑尋找算法為生成式人工智慧的說服能力奠定基礎，確保系統能夠清楚地解釋其限制和激勵，就像它們解釋路線一樣。
2603.14586.author: Ilya Ilyankou et.al.
2603.14586.authors: Ilya Ilyankou, Stefano Cavazzi, James Haworth
